skill.name: lark-contact
skill.description: Use Lark CLI to search employees and retrieve Lark contact profiles, including self lookup and open_id-based user lookup.
skill.summary: Search Lark contacts and retrieve user details through authenticated Lark CLI commands.
skill.author: larksuite

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found most were false positives from Markdown backticks, relative documentation links, and Chinese prose. The confirmed risk is medium because the skill instructs agents to run lark-cli commands that call Lark contact APIs and can expose employee directory data under the active credentials.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated Lark Contact Data Access
medium_findings.description: The skill is designed to retrieve current or specified user details and search employees by name, email, or phone through Lark APIs. This is legitimate functionality, but it may expose employee personal data if credentials, tenant scope, or organizational visibility are too broad.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation explicitly states that the commands retrieve user information and search employees using Lark contact APIs. The risk is tied to sensitive directory data access, not to hidden malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: External Lark CLI Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill requires lark-cli and provides command examples that an agent may execute. The commands appear purposeful and do not show shell interpolation, but external CLI execution can use local credentials and perform network operations.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lark-cli dependency and command examples are explicit. No evidence of command injection was found because the files document CLI arguments rather than constructing shell commands from untrusted input.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Ruby Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static Ruby backtick findings are false positives caused by Markdown inline code and fenced bash examples. The files do not contain Ruby code or backtick-based shell execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-number review shows Markdown code formatting and example commands, not executable Ruby source. The static pattern matched syntax characters without semantic context.

low_findings.title: Relative Documentation Links Misclassified as Path Traversal
low_findings.description: The path traversal findings refer to Markdown links pointing to neighboring documentation files. No file read, file write, path concatenation, or filesystem API use appears in the audited files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are documentation links such as references to shared skill instructions. They are not used as runtime paths or user-controlled filesystem operations.

low_findings.title: Entropy and Reconnaissance Heuristics Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The high-entropy, weak-cryptography, and system-reconnaissance warnings were not confirmed during review. The cited content is human-readable Chinese and Markdown command documentation, with no encoded payload, cryptographic routine, or host reconnaissance instruction found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found readable documentation and parameter descriptions at the cited areas. No suspicious encoded string, weak crypto operation, or system inventory command was present.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documented External Command Invocation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents lark-cli commands for contact lookup and search. This is the intended mechanism, but users should treat command execution as privileged because it can use local authentication and tenant access.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and central to the skill. They are not hidden or obfuscated, but they are still privileged external operations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Contact API Access Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The documentation maps commands to Lark contact and search API endpoints. This network access is expected, but it handles employee identity data and should be limited by tenant policy.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The referenced API paths are explicitly stated in the documentation. The concern is sensitive contact data exposure under authorized credentials.

content.value_statement: Finding the right Lark user can be slow when names, emails, and IDs are scattered. This skill guides authenticated Lark CLI searches and profile lookups for employee contact workflows.
actual_capabilities: Search Lark employees by keyword, including name, email, or phone number.
Retrieve the current authenticated user profile.
Retrieve a specified user profile by open_id, union_id, or user_id.
Show examples for paginated employee search results.
Explain common Lark permission errors for contact visibility.
Support table, JSON, and readable output modes documented by the CLI.
limitations: Requires lark-cli to be installed and authenticated before use.
Results depend on the active account, tenant permissions, and organization visibility.
Does not modify contacts, departments, or user records.
Does not provide its own authentication or permission management.

use_cases.title: Find a Coworker Identifier
use_cases.description: Search by name or email to find the open_id needed for Lark workflows.

use_cases.title: Check Profile Details
use_cases.description: Retrieve a user profile before routing approvals, messages, or support requests.

use_cases.title: Troubleshoot Contact Visibility
use_cases.description: Confirm whether contact lookup failures are caused by Lark organization visibility rules.

prompt_templates.title: Search by Name
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Lark contacts for the employee named Zhang San and show the most relevant results.

prompt_templates.title: Get My Profile
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Lark contact skill to retrieve my current authenticated user profile.

prompt_templates.title: Lookup by User ID
prompt_templates.prompt: Retrieve the Lark contact profile for this open_id and summarize the useful fields for a workflow handoff.

prompt_templates.title: Paginated Directory Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Lark contacts for this keyword, handle pagination if more results exist, and identify the best matching employee record.

output_examples.input: Search for a coworker by name.
output_examples.output: A short list of matching Lark users with names, identifiers, and available contact fields.

output_examples.input: Get details for a known open_id.
output_examples.output: A readable profile summary for the selected user, including the identifier type used for lookup.

output_examples.input: Investigate a permission error during lookup.
output_examples.output: An explanation that contact visibility may be restricted and that an administrator may need to adjust scope.

best_practices: Use the minimum identifier and permission scope needed for the lookup.
Confirm the active Lark tenant and account before searching employee data.
Use pagination instead of increasing page size beyond the documented limit.
anti_patterns: Do not search for employee data without a valid business reason.
Do not paste contact results into public chats or issue trackers.
Do not assume missing results mean a user does not exist; visibility rules may hide records.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It helps an agent use lark-cli to search Lark employees and retrieve user contact profiles.

faq.question: Does it create or update contacts?
faq.answer: No. The documented commands only search users and retrieve user information.

faq.question: What credentials are required?
faq.answer: It depends on an authenticated lark-cli setup with permissions for the target Lark tenant.

faq.question: Can it find users by phone or email?
faq.answer: Yes, the search workflow supports keywords such as name, email, or phone number when permissions allow it.

faq.question: Why might a lookup fail with a permission error?
faq.answer: The active account may not have organization visibility for the requested user or contact API scope.

faq.question: Is this safe for sensitive employee data?
faq.answer: Use it only under approved tenant policies because search results may contain personal or workplace contact data.
